Title: Ammonium transporter 1 increases rice resistance to sheath blight by promoting nitrogen assimilation and ethylene signalling

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AMT1;1 endogenous promoter (for pAMT1;1:Amtrac high-capacity construct); overexpression promoter not clearly specified for AMT1;1 OX
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Amino acids and chlorophyll contribute to rice resistance against sheath blight; chlorophyll also supports photosynthetic capacity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AMT1;1 promotes NH4+ uptake, nitrogen assimilation, amino acid and chlorophyll accumulation, and enhances rice resistance to sheath blight under low and medium N; AMT1;1 overexpression also improves some yield traits under low N
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Examined in leaves; function prominent under low-N and medium-N fertilization conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant identification (D358N), overexpression, RNAi, genetic combination with gs1;1, ammonium content assay, MeA sensitivity assay, pathogen inoculation, BiFC, split-ubiquitin yeast two-hybrid, yeast transport assay, qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AMT/MEP/Rh family ammonium transporter; transmembrane protein
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizoctonia solani AG1-IA infection; low, medium, and high N fertilization; NH4+ treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: AMT1;1-mediated resistance advantage is lost under high N; no major yield penalty reported, but effect is condition dependent
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A dominant-negative AMT1;1 D358N allele inhibits AMT1;1/1;2/1;3 activity. The study concludes that defense depends on ammonium assimilation rather than ammonium accumulation itself.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome data from previous study referenced in paper; accession NA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to improve nitrogen use efficiency and sheath blight resistance, especially under limited N fertilizer; should be deployed under low-N management contexts
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AMT1;1 D358N causes reduced NH4+ uptake and increased sheath blight susceptibility. 2. AMT1;1 overexpression enhances resistance, whereas AMT1 suppression reduces resistance particularly when multiple AMT1 genes are suppressed. 3. AMT1;1 promotes defense mainly through nitrogen assimilation, amino acid/chlorophyll accumulation, and activation of ethylene signaling under low N.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirect contributor to nitrogen assimilation linked to sheath blight resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AMT1;2 activity is inhibited by the dominant-negative AMT1;1 D358N protein, contributing to reduced NH4+ transport and higher susceptibility
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by NH4+ in WT; this induction is inhibited in eil1 mutant
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC, split-ubiquitin yeast two-hybrid, yeast transport inhibition assay, qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NH4+ treatment; Rhizoctonia solani infection context
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core gene because its function was directly tested in interaction and transport inhibition assays in Results.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AMT1;2 physically interacts with AMT1;1 D358N. 2. AMT1;1 D358N inhibits AMT1;2-mediated NH4+ transport in yeast. 3. AMT1;2 expression is positively influenced by EIL1 under NH4+ treatment.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirect contributor to nitrogen assimilation linked to sheath blight resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AMT1;3 activity is inhibited by the dominant-negative AMT1;1 D358N protein, contributing to reduced NH4+ transport and higher susceptibility
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC, split-ubiquitin yeast two-hybrid, yeast transport inhibition assay
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core gene because its function was directly tested in interaction and transport inhibition assays in Results.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AMT1;3 physically interacts with AMT1;1 D358N. 2. AMT1;1 D358N inhibits AMT1;3-mediated NH4+ transport in yeast. 3. Cooperative AMT1 activity contributes to disease resistance-associated N uptake.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhanced N uptake supports N assimilation-associated defense against sheath blight
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression of the high-capacity Arabidopsis ammonium transporter/sensor in rice increased NH4+ accumulation and reduced sheath blight susceptibility
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Driven by AMT1;1 promoter in rice
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression, semiquantitative RT-PCR, ammonium measurement, pathogen inoculation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used to support the conclusion that enhanced ammonium transport capacity promotes resistance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Proof-of-concept transgene for enhancing N uptake and disease resistance in rice
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pAMT1;1-driven AtAMT1;3 T464D-A141E is expressed in rice. 2. Its expression increases NH4+ accumulation. 3. Transgenic plants show reduced sheath blight susceptibility.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Zhonghua 11 (for overexpression lines); gs1;1 mutant from rice T-DNA collection
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubiquitin promoter in modified pCAMBIA1381-Ubi for overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Amino acids and chlorophyll support defense against sheath blight
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GS1;1 promotes ammonium assimilation and thereby increases rice resistance to sheath blight; loss of GS1;1 increases susceptibility and abolishes AMT1;1 OX resistance despite elevated NH4+
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant, overexpression, genetic epistasis with AMT1;1 OX, NH4+ measurement, pathogen inoculation, qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Incorporates ammonium into glutamate to form glutamine using ATP
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in ammonium assimilation; described as the key GS enzyme in rice
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for amino acids and chlorophyll synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream assimilation step connecting NH4+ uptake to downstream N metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AMT1;1 OX/gs1;1 resembling gs1;1 demonstrates that assimilation, not ammonium accumulation per se, governs resistance.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubiquitin promoter in modified pCAMBIA1381-Ubi for overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: GABA promoted hyphal growth at high concentration in this pathosystem
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of GDCi increases susceptibility to sheath blight, supporting a negative role of GABA in defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression, qRT-PCR/line verification, pathogen inoculation, exogenous GABA fungal growth assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts glutamate to GABA by decarboxylation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased GABA is associated with increased disease susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts flux from glutamate toward GABA, a metabolite associated with susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper text uses GDCi; likely a glutamate decarboxylase overexpression construct.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizoctonia solani infection; exogenous GABA treatment

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chlorophyll positively correlates with sheath blight defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of DVR function reduces chlorophyll accumulation and increases susceptibility to sheath blight
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome evidence cited for infection-responsive chlorophyll pathway genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces divinyl protochlorophyllide to the corresponding monovinyl form in chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower chlorophyll linked to pale-green phenotype and greater disease susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies direct precursor step for chlorophyll formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because mutant analysis directly linked this chlorophyll biosynthetic gene to the defense phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chlorophyll positively correlates with sheath blight defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ygl8 mutant is more susceptible to sheath blight, consistent with a positive role of chlorophyll accumulation in defense
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific catalytic assignment not described in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chlorophyll positively correlates with sheath blight defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NYC3 overexpression increases susceptibility, whereas NYC3 RNAi decreases susceptibility, indicating that chlorophyll degradation negatively affects resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi, overexpression, pathogen inoculation phenotype analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome evidence cited for infection-responsive chlorophyll catabolic genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced degradation lowers chlorophyll and increases disease susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with chlorophyll accumulation by promoting its breakdown
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Catabolic side-route opposing target metabolite accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although catabolic rather than biosynthetic, it directly controls the focal nutrient-related metabolite pool and was experimentally manipulated in Results.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Via ethylene signaling, promotes N uptake/assimilation-associated defense against sheath blight
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: EIL1 positively regulates ammonium uptake and contributes to sheath blight resistance; eil1 mutants show reduced AMT1;1/AMT1;2 induction by NH4+ and lower NH4+ accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: NH4+ treatment; ethylene signaling; nitrogen status
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to coordinate N uptake and disease resistance, especially under low-N management
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ammonium transport via feedback regulation; downstream defense-associated ethylene signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator linking ethylene signaling to NH4+ uptake and defense metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression is higher in AMT1;1 OX under low N but lower under high N, indicating nitrogen-dependent regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NH4+ treatment; low-N and high-N conditions; Rhizoctonia solani infection context
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by NH4+; higher in AMT1;1 OX and lower in AMT1 RNAi under low N
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback: ethylene signaling via EIL1 promotes AMT1 gene induction and NH4+ uptake
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Condition dependence under high N may limit utility
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or is required for NH4+-induced expression of AMT1;1 and AMT1;2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIL1 expression is positively associated with AMT1;1 activity under low N. 2. eil1 mutants show impaired NH4+-induced AMT1;1 and AMT1;2 expression. 3. eil1 mutants accumulate less NH4+, supporting a feedback role of ethylene signaling in ammonium uptake.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports ethylene signaling associated with N metabolism and sheath blight resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: EIL2 expression is higher in AMT1;1 OX and lower in AMT1 RNAi under low N, consistent with a positive role in defense-associated ethylene signaling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for improving N-responsive disease resistance, though direct genetic validation here is limited
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ethylene signaling linked to ammonium uptake/assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive ethylene-signaling regulator associated with the resistant state under low N
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct downstream targets were not validated in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-N and high-N conditions; Rhizoctonia solani infection context
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in AMT1;1 OX and lower in AMT1 RNAi under low N; reduced in AMT1;1 OX under high N
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely part of positive ethylene-associated feedforward/feedback circuitry, but not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIL2 transcript level correlates positively with AMT1;1-dependent resistance under low N. 2. Its expression decreases when AMT1 activity is reduced. 3. It is proposed to contribute to the positive ethylene signaling branch promoting defense.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Negative ethylene signaling regulator inversely associated with defense-promoting N metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ERS1 expression is suppressed in AMT1;1 OX and induced in AMT1 RNAi under low N, consistent with negative regulation of the defense-associated ethylene pathway
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential editing target to relieve repression of ethylene-mediated defense under low N
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ethylene signaling that feeds into ammonium uptake/defense programs
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator whose suppression correlates with the resistant state
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only expression association was shown; no mutant analysis in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower in AMT1;1 OX and higher in AMT1 RNAi under low N; higher in AMT1;1 OX under high N
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of ethylene signaling feedback architecture, indirect in this study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERS1 transcript abundance is inversely correlated with AMT1;1-mediated resistance under low N. 2. It is suppressed in AMT1;1 OX and induced in AMT1 RNAi. 3. The data support reduced negative ethylene signaling as part of the AMT1;1 defense mechanism.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Negative ethylene signaling regulator inversely associated with defense-promoting N metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ETR2 expression is suppressed in AMT1;1 OX and induced in AMT1 RNAi under low N, supporting a negative role in AMT1;1-associated defense signaling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential editing target to enhance low-N defense signaling, though direct causal validation is limited here
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ethylene signaling linked to N uptake and defense
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative ethylene-signaling component associated with susceptible state when elevated
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression differences disappear under high N according to the paper.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower in AMT1;1 OX and higher in AMT1 RNAi under low N; similar among genotypes under high N
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of ethylene signaling feedback architecture, indirect in this study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ETR2 transcript abundance is inversely correlated with AMT1;1-mediated resistance under low N. 2. It is suppressed in AMT1;1 OX and induced in AMT1 RNAi. 3. This supports a model where reduced negative ethylene signaling promotes defense-associated N metabolism.
